data.narrative:
Forterra is a Series B–stage defense and security company that has raised $228M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peers: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms across Defense & Security. The private peer set includes five named comparables—Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns—all at Series B stage.

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis uses the $228M total raised as a baseline and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $433M. Peer-median valuation anchors on six same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.2B and a range of $1.0B to $1.5B.

The two methods produce materially different outputs, resulting in a triangulated range of $368M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $817M. The wide spread reflects tension between the stage-progression method—which conservatively extrapolates from capital raised—and peer comparables, which suggest substantially higher valuations for defense-tech companies at this stage. Key uncertainties include whether Forterra's market position and growth trajectory align with the upper or lower end of peer valuations, and the degree to which public market comparables (2 names) validate private valuations in this sector.
